content.problemStatement: Rectify the error in the code
constraints: Strings contain only uppercase or lowercase English letters
Each string is guaranteed to have length at least 8
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single string consisting only of alphabetic characters (length between 1 and 1000).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output the 8th character (1‑based indexing) of the given string on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The only test case string is "CodeChef". Its 8th character (1‑based) is 'f'.
The 8th character of the alphabet string is 'h'.

solution.approach: For every test case we read the whole string, then directly access the character at index 7 (0‑based) which corresponds to the 8th character. No additional processing is required. This runs in O(L) time per test case where L is the length of the string, dominated by the input reading, and uses O(1) extra memory.
solution.timeComplexity: O(total length of all strings) – essentially linear in the input size.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides the input buffer.

code.Python3:
import sys
lines = sys.stdin.read().splitlines()
if not lines:
    sys.exit()
T = int(lines[0])
out = []
for i in range(1, T + 1):
    s = lines[i].strip()
    out.append(s[7])
print('\n'.join(out))
